Facts.text: Byzantine General Belisarius enters Rome while the Ostrogothic garrison peacefully leaves the city, returning the old capital to its empire
Battle of the Camel: Caliph Ali beats a rebellion near Basra led by Aisha, Mohamed's third wife
Battle of Marj Ardabil, Turkic Khazar army led by Barjik defeats Umayyad force, with 20,000 killed including general al-Jarrah ibn Abdallah, whose head is mounted on a throne
Frederick II (later also Holy Roman Emperor) crowned King of Germany in Mainz
Swiss Woudsteden renews Eternal Covenant (Oath Society)
Pope Martin V founds the University of Leuven (Netherlands), today one of Europe's oldest universities
The Geuzen (a confederacy of Calvinist Dutch nobles) under Herman de Ruyter occupies Loevestein
Netherlands & England sign military treaty
Settler Hugh Bewitt banished from Mass colony when he declares himself to be free of original sin
Dutch troops occupy harbor city Quilon (Coilan) India
Jews are expelled from Breslau Silesia
Great Britain & Netherlands sign military treaty
British parliament accepts Treaty of Paris
First execution at Newgate Jail in London (now the site of the Central Criminal Court aka the Old Bailey), relocated from Tyburn (now the site of Marble Arch)
Comet 3D/1805 V1 (Biela) approaches within 0.0366 AUs of Earth
Battle of Ayacucho (Candorcangui), Peru defeats Spain
Mikhail Glinka's opera "Russlan and Ludmilla" premieres at the Imperial Bolshoi Kamenny Theatre, in St. Petersburg, Russia
1st Young Men's Christian Association in North America set up in Montreal
The Iranian city of Bushehr surrenders to occupying British forces.
American Civil War: The Joint Committee on the Conduct of the War is established by the U.S. Congress.
Battle of Bird Creek, Indian Terr (High Shoal, Chusto-Talasah)
1st British government of Gladstone forms
Noble Order of Knights of Labor founded, Philadelphia
Russo-Turkish War: Turkish troops attempt to break Russian and Romanian encirclement of Plevna, Ottoman Empire (now Pleven, Bulgaria), fails
New Brunswick adopts Eastern Standard Time (until 1902)
Levant Richardson patents ball-bearing skate
English football club Newcastle United founded
Roman Catholics win Parliamentary election in Belgium
French activist Marguerite Durand founds the feminist daily newspaper 'La Fronde' in Paris
Boer War: During Siege of Ladysmith, Boers storm King's Post and Caesar's Camp - driven back
AL announces purchase of grounds for a stadium in NY
Norwegian parliament votes unanimiously for female suffrage
French Assembly National votes for separation of church & state
First Christmas Seals sold (Wilmington, Delaware, post office)
The German Reichstag adopts a progressive social law restricting hours of factory work by young people and women
1st US monoplane flown (Henry W Walden, Long Island, NY)
French troops occupy the Moroccan harbor city of Agadir
John K. Tener becomes president of baseball's National League
British forces under General Allenby capture Jerusalem
French troops occupies Mainz
Gabriel Narutowicz elected Polish president
Michael Hainisch re-elected Austrian president
Netherlands & Hungary trade treaty signed
Pro football a hit in NYC; Grange & Bears beat Giants before 73,000
USGA legalizes steel shaft golf clubs
Baseball cuts squad from 25 to 23 players and NL continues to prohibit uniform numbers
Benn W Levy's "Springtime for Henry" premieres in NYC
Japanese army attacks Chinese province of Jehol
Spain becomes a republic
Romania prohibits fascist Iron Guard
1st Heisman Trophy Award: Jay Berwanger, Chicago (HB)
Walter Liggett American newspaper editor and muckraker killed in gangland murder.
AL OKs night baseball for St Louis
1939 NFL Draft: Ki Aldrich from TCU first pick by Chicago Cardinals

State of California uses gas chamber instead of hanging for 2nd time in a week: Wesley Eudy and Fred Barnes executed for roles in Folsom Prison escape attempt resulting in murder of warden and guard (San Quentin State Prison, Marin County)
1940 NFL Draft: George Cafego from University of Tennessee first pick by Chicago Cardinals
Pianist and statesman Ignacy Paderewski becomes Chief of National Council of Poland (government in exile)
Russian air raid on Helsinki
British assault on Benghazi, Libya: first major allied offensive in North Africa
Illegal Jewish immigrants to Haifa are deported to Mauritius
1st US WW II bombing mission in Far East, Luzon, Philippines
7th Heisman Trophy Award: Bruce Smith, Minnesota (HB)
China declares war on Japan, Germany & Italy
Citizen Register reports "Hostile planes reported nearing Westchester"
Menahem Presser (19) makes his Carnegie Hall debut as soloist in Robert Schumann's Piano Concerto, with the Philadelphia Orchestra, under Eugene Ormandy's direction (NYC)
UN General Assembly unanimously approves Convention on Genocide
House of Representatives of The Netherlands accepts Indonesian sovereignty
NFL merges with AAFC; Cleveland Browns, San Francisco 49ers & Baltimore Colts join league intact, other teams in New York and Los Angeles are merged with existing units
West German voters approve merger of 3 states to form Baden-Wurttemberg
Great Smog of London (England): Wind resumes, lifting city's worst smog after 4 days; over 8,000 deaths attributed to conditions; clean air legislation enacted in its wake
General Electric announces all Communist employees will be fired
Montreal Canadiens (106) & Toronto Maple Leafs (98) combine for 204 penalty minutes
1st Japanese ambassador to Israel
Robert W. Welch Jr and 11 other men meet in Indianapolis to form the conservative and anti-Communist John Birch Society
1st broadcast of "Coronation Street" on British ITV
Tanganyika gains independence from Britain, takes name Tanzania - with Ukuru (Freedom) Torch lit on the summit of Mt Kilimanjaro
"I Can Get It For You Wholesale" closes on Broadway
Tanganyika becomes a republic within British Commonwealth
Nikolai Podgorny replaces Anastas Mikoyan as president of Soviet Praesidium
KRNE TV channel 12 in Merriman, NB (PBS) begins broadcasting
The Derry Citizen's Action Committee (DCAC) calls for a halt to all marches and protests for a period of one month
Dutch Antilles: government of Petronia falls
OPEC meeting in Caracas establishes 55 percent as minimum tax rate and demands that posted prices be changed to reflect changes in foreign exchange rates
Arab oil ministers announce a further production cut of 5 percent for January for non-friendly countries
NFL St. Louis Cardinal Jim Bakken kicks 6 field goals vs Atlanta Falcons
Sunningdale Agreement in Northern Ireland
Dow Jones Industrial Average hits 570.01
Johnson Grigsby freed after 66 years in jail in Indiana
Iraq completes nationalization by taking over the BP, CFP, and Shell shares of the Basrah Petroleum Company
1st game of Women's Pro Basketball League (WBL), Chicago Hustle vs Milwaukee Does
Pioneer Venus 2 drops 5 probes into atmosphere of Venus
61°F in Boston at 1 AM
World Boxing Council first to reduce the length of championship fights from 15 to 12 rounds, and allows referees to order standing 8-count for fighters in trouble
Counselor toRonald Reagan, Edwin Meese says people go to soup kitchens "because food is free & that's easier than paying for it"
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Great Britain performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Iranian commandos end 5-day hijacking of Kuwaiti plane
Phoenix, Arizona, gets 3" of snow
Palestine uprising begin in Israeli-occupied West Bank
NY Yankees sign 12-year television contract with MSG for $500M
Premiere of fact based crime drama film "Mississippi Burning"
The Michael Hughes Bridge in Sligo, Ireland is officially opened.
Gunda Niemann skates ladies world record 3k ladies (4:10.80)
Houston scores most points against Cleveland, Oilers 58, Browns 14
NFL NY Giant Otis Anderson becomes 8th to rush for 10,000th yard
Cincinnati Reds owner Marge Schott apologizes for racist remarks
NHL NJ Devils organization announces that it will change its colors to black, red, & white for the 1992-93 season
Sri Lanka's third-ever Test Cricket victory (v NZ by 9 wkts)
Ivory Coast Premier Ouattara resigns
5m meteor 1994 XM1 passes within 100,000 km of Earth
US Surgeon General Joycelyn Elders resigns after comments about masturbation
The Messel Pit in Germany inscribed on UNECO's World Heritage List as the world richest fossil site for Eocene period (57-36 million years ago)
Tottenham defender Ledley King scores fastest goal in English Premier League history when he nets after 9.82 seconds in 3-3 draw at Bradford City
A blast in the center of Moscow kills six people and wounds several more.
72nd Heisman Trophy Award: Troy Smith, Ohio State (QB)
Jigme Singye Wangchuk, King of Bhutan abdicates the throne
Moscow suffers its worst fire since 1977, killing 45 women in a drug rehabitational center.
13 people are killed and 10 are injured after a bus rolls of a cliff and falls 100 meters in Taiwan
6 people are killed and 41 are injured after a bus plunges 30 metres down a gorge in Guatemala
Incumbent Ghanaian president John Mahama wins the general election amidst allegations of fraud
The Social Liberal Union Party and Romanian Prime Minister Victor Ponta win by a landslide in the parliamentary elections
MLB pitcher Roy Halladay announces his retirement due to persistent back injury
CIA Torture Report released, detailing the CIA's use of torture on detainees between 2001-2006
Amazon's best-selling book of the year is "Girl on a Train" by Paula Hawkins
Attack on Kandahar airport by Taliban forces kills at least 37 before Afghan forces retake control
Ghanaian Presidential election won by Nana Akufo-Addo of the opposition New Patriotic Party
Mosconi Cup nine-ball pool, London, England: Europe wins 7th consecutive title convincingly beating US, 11-3; MVP: Albin Ouschan (Austria)
83rd Heisman Trophy Award: Baker Mayfield, Oklahoma (QB)
Funeral for French rock star Johnny Hallyday draws hundreds of thousands onto Paris streets
Same-sex marriage is legalized in Australia after being signed into law by the Governor General
Armenia's acting Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan wins a snap election with 70.4% of the vote

Copa Libertadores Final, Madrid: River Plate beats Boca Juniors 3-1 in extra time (aggregate 5-3); Juan Quintero's stunning extra-time strike helps River come from a goal down to record historic victory over 10-man Boca
Tens of thousands march on Delhi, India, to demand Hindi temple be built on contentious religious site in Ayodhya
Winter storm hits US south east killing three, leaving hundreds of thousands without power, cancelling over 1,000 flights
Chilean Air Force plane lost on flight to Antarctica with presumed loss of 38 lives
US officials "deliberately misled" the public on progress of the Afghanistan war, hid that it was a lost cause, according to The Washington Post analysis of the "Afghanistan Papers"
White Island / Whakaari volcano, a tourist attraction in New Zealand, erupts killing 22
World Anti-Doping Agency bans Russia from all major sporting events including 2020 Tokyo Olympics and 2022 football World Cup in Qatar for manipulating laboratory data
Canada approves the Pfizer/ BioNTech vaccine for COVID-19
United Arab Emirates is the first country to authorize China's Sinopharm vaccine for COVID-19 saying it is 86% effective
Actor Jussie Smollett found guilty of falsely reporting a hate crime in 2019, in a Chicago court (overturned 2024)
Chicago Black Hawks Marc-Andre Fleury becomes 3rd NHL goaltender to reach 500 career wins in 2-0 shut-out over the Canadiens at Montreal
More than 40 camels disqualified from the King Abdulaziz Camel Festival beauty contest after Botox injections and other cosmetic enhancements were discovered
Truck with a trailer carrying 150 migrants crashes in Chiapas, Mexico, killing 54
89th Heisman Trophy Award: Jayden Daniels, LSU (QB)
Google unveils a new quantum computer, capable of performing a mathematical calculation in 5 minutes, that the most powerful supercomputers could not complete in 10 septillion years
Luigi Mangione (26) is arrested at an Altoona, Pennsylvania fast food restaurant after a five-day manhunt, and charged with murder of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson in NYC
NFL Philadelphia Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets new franchise season record for yards rushing, 1,623 yards with four games still to play
KingJames II's wife and son flee England for France
Colley Cibber's play "Caesar in Egypt" premieres in London
At Fort Ticonderoga, New York,Henry Knoxbegins his historic transport of abandoned British artillery to Boston, Massachusetts, later forces the British to evacuate Boston
Noah Websterestablishes New York's 1st daily newspaper, the "American Minerva"
Alfred Tennyson's poem "Charge of the Light Brigade" is published in "The Examiner"
Joseph Pulitzerbuys the St. Louis Dispatch for $2,500
US PresidentBenjamin Harrisondedicates the Chicago Auditorium, designed byLouis Sullivanand Dankmar Adler, then largest building in the US
PresidentPaul Krugerof South Africa's Transvaal & QueenWilhelmina of the Netherlandshave a triumphant procession
Richard Strauss' opera "Salome" premieres in Dresden
NY American reports Belgian KingLeopold IIbribed US Senate commission on the Congo
Gustav Mahlerdeparts Vienna for America
Heavyweight fight betweenJack Johnsonand Jim Johnson ends with no decision in Paris
AL extendsBan Johnson's contract to 1935 and raises is salary to $40,000
300 Montgomery, San Francisco opens as newBank of AmericaHQ
Adolf Hitlerorders US ships and those loyal to her to be torpedoed - 3 days before declaring war on the US
Dmitri Shostakovichappointed honored guest of Royal Swedish Academy of Music
John Coltrane's Quartet records their greatest work "A Love Supreme" at Van Gelder Studio in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey
"ACharlie BrownChristmas", the first Peanuts animated special premieres on CBS in the US
Frank Robinsonis traded from the Cincinnati Reds to the Baltimore Orioles
Final performances of soul singerOtis Redding:  WEWS -TV
Jim Morrisonarrested on stage for disturbing the peace at the New Haven Arena, Connecticut, making him the 1st rock star to be taken into custody during a performance
Nicolae Ceaușescu becomes President of Romania (overthrown in 1989)

Douglas Engelbartdemonstrates in "The Mother of All Demos" the computer system NLS (oN-Line System) to a live audience in San Francisco. Shows for the first time, the mouse, word processing, windows, hypertext links, video conferencing, real-time collaboration, and other modern computing concepts.
Terence O'Neill, Northern Ireland Prime Minister, makes a television appeal for moderate opinion in what became known as the 'Ulster stands at the Crossroads' speech

Fantasy Records releases "Pendulum", the sixth studio album by American rock bandCreedence ClearwaterRevival; all the songs, including the singles "Have You Ever Seen the Rain?" and "Hey Tonight" are written byJohn Fogerty
Lewis F. Powell Jr.appointed to the US Supreme Court

Howard Cosell briefly interviews rockerJohn Lennon, and Frank Gifford chats with California governorRonald Reagan, during halftime of Monday Night Football broadcast from the Coliseum  in Los Angeles, California
Jack Briscobeats Giant Baba in Tokyo, to become NWA wrestling champ
US PresidentGerald Fordsigns $2.3 New York City Seasonal Financing Act amid fiscal crisis in the city
LA RamEric Dickersonrushes 215 yards for season record 2,105 yards
Argentinian Junta leadersJorge Rafael Videlaand Emilio Eduardo Massera sentenced to life imprisonment for human rights violations in Buenos Aires
SF 49ers wide receiverJerry Ricebegins NFL streak of 274 consecutive games with a reception in a 27-20 loss to LA Rams at Candlestick Park
Larry Birdends streak of 59 consecutive free throws
Lech Wałęsawins the first direct presidential election in Poland
61st Heisman Trophy Award:Eddie George, Ohio State (RB)
"Tomorrow Never Dies", 18th James Bond film starringPierce BrosnanandMichelle Yeoh, premieres in London
"Star Trek: Nemesis" film directed by Stuart Baird, and starringPatrick StewartandJonathan Frakespremieres
"Peter Pan" live action adventure film adaptation of the 1904 play and 1911 by J.M. Barrie premieres in London; flops at the box office
Mary-Kate Olsenpledges that woman sewing garments for her company in Bangladesh will be afforded legal maternity leave
"Brokeback Mountain" film released, directed byAng Lee, starringHeath LedgerandJake Gyllenhaal, based on story by Annie Proulx
Viacom's Paramount Pictures division agrees to pay $1.6 billion for DreamWorks SKG, the Hollywood studio founded bySteven Spielberg, Jeffrey Katzenberg, andDavid Geffen

Governor of Illinois, Rod Blagojevich, is arrested by federal officials for a number of alleged crimes, including attempting to sell the United States Senate seat being vacated by President-electBarack Obama
Juan Manuel Márquez knocks outManny Pacquiaoin round six for the WBO light welterweight title
Mike Ditka's #89 jersey is retired by the Chicago Bears
German ChancellorAngela Merkelnamed Time Magazine's Person of the Year, for her handling of debt and refugee crises
South Korean MPs vote to impeach PresidentPark Geun-hye
"Star Wars: The Last Jedi" directed by Rian Johnson, starringMark Hamill,Carrie Fisher,Daisy Ridley,John BoyegaandAdam Driverpremieres in Los Angeles
Brian Ortegadefeats Cub Swanson at UFC Fight Night 123, earning Fight of the Night and Performance of the Night honors
Iraqi Prime MinisterHaider al-Abadideclares victory over the Islamic State in Iraq, ending more than 3 years of conflict

Green Bay quarterbackAaron RodgersbreaksTom Brady's NFL record with his 359th straight pass without an interception during Packers 34-20 win over Atlanta Falcons; finishes game with streak intact at 368

New England Patriots quarterbackTom Bradybreaks Peyton Manning's record for most touchdown passes in NFL history; moves to 582 with 3 TD passes during 34-33 defeat by Miami Dolphins; record includes TDs thrown in regular season & post-season

Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at The Beacon Theatre, NYC; performers include:Sting,Bruce Springsteen,James Taylor,John Mellencamp,Bob Geldof,Ricky Martin,Eurythmics, Deborah Harry,Shaggy, Zucchero, MC Rodriguez, and H.E.R.
Dark fantasy game "Elden Ring", created byHidetaka Miyazakiand George RR Martin, awarded Game of the Year at the Game Awards
Sheet of paper on which scientistCharles Darwindefended his theory of evolution in 1865 sells for a record $882,000 at Sotheby's
Japanese baseball two-way superstarShohei Ohtanisigns a North American pro-sports record 10-year $700m deal with the Los Angeles Dodgers
The first traffic lights are installed outside the Palace of Westminster in London. Resembling railway signals, they use semaphore arms and are illuminated at night by red and green gas lamps.

US Marines and allied nations launch an amphibious and airborne operation in Mogadishu, Somalia to restore order to the war-torn nation. Authorized by UN Security Council Resolution 794 passed on December 3.
